cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
cees: noun. plural of cee, the name of the letter C.
cue: noun. The name of the Latin script letter Q / q.
ecu: noun. The European Currency Unit (symbol ₠), a currency used in the European Community before the euro.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
ensue: verb. To follow (a leader, inclination etc.).
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
queen: noun. The wife, consort, or widow of a king.
scene: noun. The location of an event that attracts attention.
see: verb. To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
sequence: noun. A set of things next to each other in a set order; a series.
sue: verb. To file a legal action against someone, generally a non-criminal (civil) action.
sun: noun. A star, especially when seen as the centre of any single solar system.
cns: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Asmat.
eques: noun. A member of the equestrian order (Latin: ordo equester), the lower of the two aristocratic classes of Ancient Rome, ranking below the
esne: noun. A hireling of servile status; slave.
esq: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Esselen.
que: noun. The name of the Latin script letter Q / q.
scun: verb. Alternative form of scoon (“to skim along a surface”).
unc: adjective. Old; no longer versed in or relevant to current youth culture.
unce: noun. Early Modern spelling of ounce.
unsee: verb. To undo the act of seeing something; to erase the memory of having seen something, or otherwise reverse the effect of having seen
usee: noun. One who or that which is used.
